THIS WEEK IN LOCAL GOVERNMENT

Kronenwetter launched a new Parks Committee with plans for a major playground overhaul at Bushka Park, while the Wausau School Board and its teachers union opened contract negotiations for the coming school year.

  • Kronenwetter's new Parks Committee elected Erin Mishka as chair and began planning a $150,000–$200,000 playground replacement at Bushka Park, with a July 17 movie-under-the-stars fundraiser in the works.
  • The Wausau Board of Education and the Wausau Education Association exchanged initial contract proposals for 2026-27 at a public meeting July 1.
  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee is set to vote on revisions to the village's Chapter 382 nuisances ordinance at its July 6 meeting.
  • Kronenwetter's CLIPP Committee is also set to consider traffic control measures on County Road XX and Terrebonne Drive on July 6.
  • Kronenwetter's Parks Committee scheduled a public parks tour for Wednesday, July 8 at 4 p.m. at the municipal center, with July 9 as a rain date.

Coming up: The Village of Weston's Board of Trustees and Community Life & Public Safety Committee both meet Monday, July 7, and Wausau's Economic Development Committee meets Tuesday evening — while Marathon County's Infrastructure Committee and two City of Wausau committees convene Thursday, July 9.

Full summaries on the Meeting Tracker →
